person_name: Avitus of Vienne, Bishop of Vienne (c. 494–518)
person_name_full: Avitus of Vienne, Bishop of Vienne (c. 494–518)
notes: Bishop of Vienne circa 494–518, known for pastoral letters and moral guidance.

docs.title: Letter 2008
docs.author: Avitus of Vienne
docs.excerpt: Avitus writes to the Pope as both a loyal supporter of Roman authority and a Gallic bishop navigating his own complex relationships with the Burgundian court.

docs.title: Letter 3018
docs.author: Avitus of Vienne
docs.excerpt: … in both cities equally, but to your devoted father, as far as is fitting, until we can follow you wherever you go, grant us the patience of those who wait in hope.

docs.title: Letter 3
docs.author: Symmachus (Pope)
docs.subject_summary:
Ep. 4: 
a.  501  d.         Sf mmaclii  papac  ad  Ayituni  episcopum  Yiennensein.

Citr  inaudUam  causam  ipsius  judicare
docs.excerpt: … because Avitus of Vienne is a man of genuine integrity and because the Gallic bishops have a role to play in the resolution of this crisis. Their recognition of the legitimate bishop of Rome matters; their refusal to recognize a schismatic claimant matters equally.
